access_log.desc: Sets the path and format for access logs. Proper configuration can alleviate disk IO bottlenecks, optimize log recording, and improve log readability and analysis efficiency. Multiple logs can be specified at the same configuration level.

aio_write.desc: The aio_write parameter specifies whether to use asynchronous IO (aio) for writing files. Setting to 'on' enables asynchronous writing, which can alleviate disk IO bottlenecks; setting to 'off' disables asynchronous writing. This parameter is suitable for scenarios requiring optimized file write performance.

client_body_buffer_size.desc: Sets the buffer size for client request bodies. This parameter defines the buffer size for reading client request bodies. If the request body is larger than the buffer, the entire request body or part of it will be written to a temporary file. Increasing this value can handle larger request bodies but increases memory usage, potentially helping to alleviate memory bottlenecks.

client_header_buffer_size.desc: Sets the buffer size for client request headers. For most requests, a 1K byte buffer is sufficient. Increasing this value can handle larger request headers but increases memory usage, suitable for alleviating memory bottlenecks.

directio_alignment.desc: Sets the alignment bytes for direct I/O. This parameter optimizes disk I/O performance. Increasing this value improves alignment performance suitable for large file processing, while decreasing it is better for small file processing. Proper configuration of this parameter can alleviate disk I/O bottlenecks.

gzip.desc: Enables or disables gzip compression of response content, which can improve network transmission efficiency and alleviate network bottlenecks. Values are 'on' (enable) or 'off' (disable). When enabled, Nginx compresses response content to reduce data transmission volume.

gzip_buffers.desc: Sets the number and size of buffers used for compressing responses. Increasing this value improves Gzip compression performance but occupies more memory, suitable for alleviating memory bottlenecks.

gzip_comp_level.desc: Sets the gzip compression level for responses, ranging from 1 to 9. Increasing this value improves compression ratio but increases CPU load; decreasing it reduces compression ratio but improves speed. This parameter can alleviate network bottlenecks.

gzip_http_version.desc: Sets the supported HTTP version for Gzip compression, typically 1.0 or 1.1. Choosing 1.1 supports more features but may affect compatibility. This parameter helps alleviate network bottlenecks.

gzip_min_length.desc: Sets the minimum length of response content for Gzip compression in bytes. This length is determined by the 'Content-Length' response header field. Increasing this value avoids compressing small files, saving CPU resources and alleviating network bottlenecks. Recommended to set above 20 bytes to ensure effective compression.

gzip_vary.desc: Enables or disables inserting the 'Vary: Accept-Encoding' response header field. This setting helps proxy servers cache different responses based on client compression support, thereby alleviating network bottlenecks. When enabled, the response header includes Vary: Accept-Encoding field, allowing better cache management and content distribution.

keepalive_disable.desc: Used to disable keep-alive functionality for certain user agents to prevent persistent connections with poorly performing browsers. Can alleviate memory bottlenecks. Use the 'browser' parameter to specify affected browsers, with multiple values separated by spaces.

keepalive_requests.desc: Sets the maximum number of requests that can be served through one keep-alive connection. This parameter can alleviate memory bottlenecks. Increasing this value improves connection reuse rate, but setting it too high may cause excessive resource usage. Adjust this value appropriately to optimize performance.

keepalive_time.desc: Limits the maximum time a keep-alive connection remains open before being closed. Increasing this value reduces connection closing frequency, thereby alleviating memory bottlenecks, but may increase resource usage.

keepalive_timeout.desc: The keepalive_timeout parameter sets the timeout for client keep-alive connections. After exceeding this time, the connection will be closed. This parameter can alleviate memory bottlenecks. Increasing this value reduces frequent connection establishment and closure. Default value is 75 seconds.

limit_rate_after.desc: Sets the time after transmission starts when rate limiting takes effect, in bytes. This parameter controls the initial transmission volume before rate limiting takes effect after response transmission to the client. Increasing this value allows users to enjoy higher download speeds initially before bandwidth is limited, thereby alleviating network bottlenecks.

lingering_close.desc: Controls how Nginx closes client connections. This parameter can be set to 'on', 'off', or 'always'. When set to 'on', Nginx waits for a period when closing connections to allow clients to complete data transmission, thereby alleviating network bottlenecks. When set to 'off', connections close immediately, while 'always' performs delayed processing every time a connection is closed. Proper configuration of this parameter can improve server performance and user experience.

lingering_time.desc: Specifies the maximum time for nginx to process additional data from clients when closing connections. Increasing this value improves connection closing stability, while decreasing it is suitable for high concurrency scenarios, alleviating network bottlenecks.

lingering_timeout.desc: Specifies the maximum time to wait for more client data to arrive when closing connections. Increasing this value improves connection closing stability, while decreasing it is suitable for high concurrency scenarios. This parameter can alleviate network bottlenecks.

open_file_cache.desc: Configures a cache to store open file descriptors, file sizes and modification times, directory existence information, and file lookup errors. Enabling this parameter improves Nginx file access performance and reduces disk I/O bottlenecks. Values can be OFF (disable cache), ON (enable cache), or a specific cache size. When cache is enabled, Nginx caches open file handles in memory, reducing file system access.

open_file_cache_errors.desc: This parameter enables or disables caching of file lookup errors for open_file_cache. By caching errors, frequent file opening operations due to file errors can be reduced, thereby alleviating disk IO bottlenecks. Recommended for scenarios requiring frequent file access. Set to 'off' to disable this function.

open_file_cache_min_uses.desc: Sets the minimum number of uses to retain file handles in cache. This parameter defines the minimum number of times a file is accessed within the time period configured by the inactive parameter of the open_file_cache directive. Increasing this value allows less frequently used file handles to be removed faster, freeing memory and alleviating disk IO bottlenecks.

open_file_cache_valid.desc: Sets the valid time for cached file handles in seconds. Increasing this value reduces the frequency of file handle reopening, thereby alleviating disk IO bottlenecks and reducing system burden. Recommended to adjust this value according to actual requirements to optimize performance.

output_buffers.desc: Sets the number and size of buffers used for reading responses from disk. Increasing this value improves concurrent performance, suitable for high traffic scenarios; decreasing it is better for low traffic scenarios. This parameter can alleviate memory bottlenecks.

postpone_output.desc: This parameter sets the number of bytes that can be postponed before sending responses. If possible, client data transmission will be postponed until nginx has at least the specified number of bytes of data to send. Increasing this value improves network utilization, suitable for high bandwidth scenarios; decreasing it is better for low latency scenarios to alleviate network bottlenecks.

proxy_buffering.desc: Enables or disables buffering of responses from proxy servers. Enabling improves performance but increases memory usage, suitable for alleviating memory bottlenecks. Value 'ON' enables, 'OFF' disables.

proxy_buffers.desc: Sets the number and size of buffers used for reading responses from proxy servers. This parameter helps alleviate memory bottlenecks. Increasing this value handles larger responses but increases memory usage. Recommended to adjust according to actual requirements.

proxy_buffer_size.desc: Sets the buffer size for reading the first part of responses received from proxy servers. Increasing this value handles larger responses, helping alleviate memory bottlenecks but increases memory usage. Recommended to adjust according to actual requirements.

proxy_busy_buffers_size.desc: When buffering responses from proxy servers is enabled, limits the total size of buffers that can be busy sending responses to clients. Increasing this value improves performance but increases memory usage. Recommended to adjust according to actual situations to alleviate memory bottlenecks.

proxy_cache_min_uses.desc: Sets the minimum number of cache uses. This parameter controls the validity of cached content. Increasing this value ensures only frequently requested content is cached, thereby alleviating disk IO bottlenecks; decreasing it may cause less frequently used content to be cached. Proper setting of this parameter improves cache efficiency and performance.

proxy_cache_use_stale.desc: This parameter determines under what circumstances expired cached responses can be used when communicating with proxy servers. Enabling this option alleviates disk IO bottlenecks, reduces backend requests, and improves response speed, but may return expired data. Optional values include: error, timeout, invalid_header, updating, http_500, http_502, http_503, http_504, http_403, http_404, http_429, and off.

proxy_connect_timeout.desc: Defines the timeout for establishing connections with proxy servers in seconds. This timeout helps alleviate network bottlenecks. Increasing this value avoids connection failures due to network latency, while decreasing it speeds up failure detection. Note that this timeout usually cannot exceed 75 seconds.

proxy_ignore_client_abort.desc: Determines whether to close connections with proxy servers when clients close connections. When set to 'ON', the proxy server ignores client abort requests, ensuring backend continues processing requests, thus improving backend processing stability and alleviating network bottlenecks. When set to 'OFF', the proxy server does not ignore client abort requests.

proxy_max_temp_file_size.desc: Sets the maximum allowed temporary file size for handling responses that don't fit in buffers. Increasing this value alleviates disk IO bottlenecks and handles larger responses but increases disk usage. Recommended to adjust according to actual requirements.

proxy_read_timeout.desc: Defines the timeout for reading backend server responses from proxy servers. This parameter helps alleviate network bottlenecks and prevents long-unresponsive connections from occupying resources. Value is time in seconds. Increasing this value handles slower backend responses; decreasing it releases resources faster.

proxy_request_buffering.desc: Enables or disables buffering of client request bodies. Enabling buffering improves performance and reduces memory usage, suitable for high concurrency scenarios. Disabling buffering may reduce memory usage but could affect performance. Values are 'ON' or 'OFF', where 'ON' enables buffering and 'OFF' disables buffering.

proxy_send_timeout.desc: Sets the timeout for proxy servers to send responses to clients. This parameter controls the timeout for sending requests to proxied servers, effectively alleviating network bottlenecks and preventing long-unresponsive connections from occupying resources. Value is time in seconds. Increasing this value handles slower client connections; decreasing it releases resources faster.

proxy_temp_file_write_size.desc: Sets the buffer size for writing to temporary files. Increasing this value improves performance, especially under high load, helping alleviate disk IO bottlenecks but increases disk usage. Recommended to adjust according to actual situations to optimize performance and resource usage.

read_ahead.desc: Sets the number of bytes to read ahead. This parameter causes the kernel to read ahead the specified number of bytes when processing files to alleviate disk IO bottlenecks. Increasing this value improves read performance, suitable for large files; decreasing it is better for small files. Proper configuration of this parameter optimizes system file reading efficiency.

reset_timedout_connection.desc: This parameter sets whether to reset timed-out connections and connections closed with non-standard code 444. Enabling this function alleviates memory bottlenecks and releases resources faster. Value 'ON' enables, 'OFF' disables.

sendfile.desc: Enables or disables the sendfile() system call for efficiently sending file content to network connections. This function significantly improves file transfer efficiency and reduces disk I/O bottlenecks. Value 'ON' enables, 'OFF' disables. By default, this parameter is 'OFF'.

sendfile_max_chunk.desc: Sets the maximum bytes sent per call in the sendfile() system call. Increasing this value improves large file transfer efficiency; decreasing it is better for small files. This parameter alleviates disk I/O bottlenecks and optimizes file transfer performance.

send_timeout.desc: Sets the timeout for sending responses. This parameter helps alleviate network bottlenecks. Increasing this value improves long connection stability, suitable for scenarios requiring long-term connection maintenance; while decreasing it is better for short connection scenarios to improve resource utilization and response speed.

tcp_nodelay.desc: Enables or disables the TCP_NODELAY option. This option is enabled when connections enter keep-alive state. Additionally, it is enabled in SSL connections, unbuffered proxies, and WebSocket proxies. Enabling this option alleviates network bottlenecks and improves performance.

tcp_nopush.desc: This parameter controls whether to enable the TCP_NOPUSH (on FreeBSD) or TCP_CORK (on Linux) option when using sendfile. These options combine multiple small data packets into one large data packet when sending data, reducing network latency and alleviating network bottlenecks. Value 'on' enables, 'off' disables.
